title: Server-Side Request Forgery
description: An application that fetches metadata from a provided URL.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify the vulnerability and what an attacker can achieve.
stages.target: The server fetches a URL provided by the user without validation, allowing the attacker to make the server send requests to internal resources.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a URL to access the AWS metadata service (169.254.169.254).

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: How would you mitigate this using an allowlist?
stages.target: Validate the URL against a list of approved domains or IP addresses before making the request.

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Why is SSRF particularly dangerous in cloud environments?
stages.target: Cloud environments often have metadata services (like AWS, GCP, Azure) that can provide sensitive information like IAM credentials or configuration data.

hints: The server is doing a request on your behalf.
Internal IP addresses might be accessible from the server but not from the internet.
